title: Xenial Charred Malaysian Sweetcorn Fried-Rice
description: A cozy Malaysian-inspired fried rice featuring charred sweetcorn, smoky tofu, and warm spices, staying satvik-friendly.
instructions: Heat a large wok or skillet over medium-high heat.
Add coconut oil and swirl to coat the pan.
Add smoked tofu cubes and fry until golden and slightly charred, about 2 minutes.
Stir in the sweetcorn kernels and continue frying for another 2 minutes.
Push the tofu and corn to one side of the pan, pour in a bit more oil if needed, then add the cooked rice.
Sprinkle turmeric, coriander, and cumin over the rice and stir-fry everything together for 4 minutes, ensuring the rice gets a light char.
Drizzle lime juice over the mixture and toss in chopped cilantro, seasoning with salt to taste.
Remove from heat and serve hot, optionally garnishing with extra cilantro and lime wedges.
